```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,212 @@
- 
+ # Cursor IDE Rules for Maktab Project
+ 
+ ## Project Context
+ This is a monorepo timetable/scheduling application with:
+ - **Backend**: TypeScript + Express + TypeORM (packages/api)
+ - **Frontend**: React 19 + Vite + TanStack Router (packages/web) - RTL-first Persian/Dari UI
+ - **Solver**: Python 3.x with OR-Tools (packages/solver)
+ - **Desktop**: Electron shell
+ 
+ ## Code Style &amp; Conventions
+ 
+ ### TypeScript
+ - Use **strict mode** (already configured in tsconfig.json)
+ - Prefer **explicit types** over &#96;any&#96;
+ - Use **JSDoc comments** for public APIs and complex functions
+ - Follow **camelCase** for variables/functions, **PascalCase** for classes/types
+ - Use **Zod schemas** for runtime validation (already in use)
+ - Prefer **interfaces** for object shapes, **types** for unions/intersections
+ 
+ ### React/Frontend
+ - Use **functional components** with hooks
+ - Follow **RTL-first** approach (Persian/Dari primary language)
+ - Use **Tailwind logical properties** (&#96;ms-4&#96; instead of &#96;ml-4&#96;, &#96;text-start&#96; instead of &#96;text-left&#96;)
+ - Prefer **TanStack Query** for server state, **Zustand** for UI state
+ - Use **React Hook Form + Zod** for form validation
+ - Follow **shadcn/ui** component patterns
+ 
+ ### File Organization
+ - **Feature-based** structure in &#96;packages/web/src/features/&#96;
+ - **Co-locate** related files (components, hooks, types)
+ - Use **index.ts** for clean exports
+ - Separate **UI components** (&#96;components/ui/&#96;) from **feature components** (&#96;features/*/&#96;)
+ 
+ ### Naming Conventions
+ - Components: &#96;PascalCase.tsx&#96; (e.g., &#96;ScheduleBoard.tsx&#96;)
+ - Hooks: &#96;camelCase.ts&#96; with &#96;use&#96; prefix (e.g., &#96;useScheduleValidation.ts&#96;)
+ - Utilities: &#96;camelCase.ts&#96; (e.g., &#96;date.ts&#96;, &#96;validation.ts&#96;)
+ - Types/Schemas: &#96;camelCase.ts&#96; (e.g., &#96;common.types.ts&#96;, &#96;subject.schema.ts&#96;)
+ 
+ ### Import Order
+ 1. External dependencies (React, libraries)
+ 2. Internal utilities (&#96;@/lib/...&#96;)
+ 3. Components (&#96;@/components/...&#96;)
+ 4. Types (&#96;@/types/...&#96;)
+ 5. Relative imports (&#96;./...&#96;)
+ 
+ ### Comments &amp; Documentation
+ - Use **JSDoc** for exported functions/classes
+ - Include **@example** for complex usage
+ - Document **RTL considerations** when relevant
+ - Add **TODO comments** with context when needed
+ 
+ ## Technology-Specific Guidelines
+ 
+ ### TanStack Router
+ - Use **file-based routing** in &#96;packages/web/src/routes/&#96;
+ - Leverage **type-safe** route params and search params
+ - Use **route loaders** for data fetching
+ 
+ ### TanStack Query
+ - Use **query keys** as arrays: &#96;['teachers', id]&#96;
+ - Set appropriate **staleTime** (5min for reference data)
+ - Use **mutations** for create/update/delete operations
+ 
+ ### Zustand
+ - Keep stores **focused** (one store per domain: &#96;scheduleStore&#96;, &#96;uiStore&#96;)
+ - Use **immer** pattern for complex state updates if needed
+ 
+ ### Tailwind CSS
+ - Use **CSS variables** from Naqsh design system (see FRONTEND_ARCHITECTURE.md)
+ - Prefer **logical properties** for RTL support
+ - Use **arbitrary values** sparingly: &#96;w-[200px]&#96; only when needed
+ 
+ ### Zod Validation
+ - Define schemas in &#96;schemas/&#96; directory
+ - Reuse base schemas, extend with &#96;.extend()&#96; or &#96;.merge()&#96;
+ - Use **refinement** for complex validation rules
+ 
+ ## RTL (Right-to-Left) Considerations
+ 
+ ### Critical Rules
+ - **Always** use logical properties: &#96;ms-*&#96;, &#96;me-*&#96;, &#96;ps-*&#96;, &#96;pe-*&#96;, &#96;start-*&#96;, &#96;end-*&#96;
+ - **Never** use &#96;left-*&#96;, &#96;right-*&#96;, &#96;ml-*&#96;, &#96;mr-*&#96; for layout
+ - Test UI in **both RTL and LTR** modes
+ - Use &#96;dir="rtl"&#96; on &#96;&lt;html&gt;&#96; for Persian/Dari
+ - Consider **bidi text** in mixed-language content
+ 
+ ### Fonts
+ - **Vazirmatn** for Persian/Arabic text
+ - **Inter** for Latin/English text
+ - **JetBrains Mono** for code/monospace
+ 
+ ### Numerals
+ - Use **Eastern Arabic numerals** (۰۱۲۳) in Persian UI
+ - Use **Western numerals** (0123) in English UI and code
+ 
+ ## API Integration
+ 
+ ### Backend API Structure
+ - Base URL: &#96;http://localhost:3000/api&#96; (dev)
+ - Use **RESTful** conventions: &#96;GET /api/teachers&#96;, &#96;POST /api/teachers&#96;, etc.
+ - Follow existing backend patterns in &#96;packages/api/src/routes/&#96;
+ 
+ ### Error Handling
+ - Use **TanStack Query error boundaries**
+ - Display **user-friendly** error messages in Persian/English
+ - Log **technical errors** to console in development
+ 
+ ## Testing
+ 
+ ### Backend (packages/api)
+ - Use **Vitest** for unit tests
+ - Test files: &#96;*.test.ts&#96; or &#96;*.property.test.ts&#96;
+ - Coverage target: Aim for 80%+ on critical paths
+ 
+ ### Frontend (packages/web)
+ - Use **Vitest + React Testing Library** (when added)
+ - Test **user interactions**, not implementation details
+ - Mock **TanStack Query** for API calls
+ 
+ ## Performance
+ 
+ ### Frontend Optimizations
+ - Use **React.memo** for expensive components (grid cells)
+ - Implement **code splitting** via TanStack Router lazy loading
+ - Use **virtual scrolling** for long lists if needed
+ - Optimize **drag-and-drop** with &#96;@dnd-kit&#96; best practices
+ 
+ ### Backend Optimizations
+ - Use **TypeORM query builder** for complex queries
+ - Implement **caching** for reference data (teachers, subjects)
+ - Use **pagination** for large datasets
+ 
+ ## Git &amp; Version Control
+ 
+ ### Commit Messages
+ - Use **conventional commits**: &#96;feat:&#96;, &#96;fix:&#96;, &#96;docs:&#96;, &#96;refactor:&#96;, etc.
+ - Include **scope** when relevant: &#96;feat(web): add schedule board component&#96;
+ - Reference **issues** when applicable: &#96;fix(api): resolve duplicate subjects #123&#96;
+ 
+ ### Branch Strategy
+ - &#96;main&#96;: Production-ready code
+ - &#96;develop&#96;: Integration branch
+ - Feature branches: &#96;feature/description&#96; or &#96;feat/description&#96;
+ 
+ ## Environment Variables
+ 
+ ### Frontend (.env)
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api
+ VITE_APP_VERSION=$npm_package_version
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Backend (.env)
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ NODE_ENV=development
+ LOG_LEVEL=debug
+ LOG_FORMAT=json
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Common Patterns
+ 
+ ### API Client Pattern
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+ // lib/api.ts
+ export const api = {
+   teachers: {
+     list: () =&gt; queryClient.fetchQuery({ queryKey: ['teachers'], queryFn: () =&gt; fetch('/api/teachers').then(r =&gt; r.json()) }),
+     // ...
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Form Pattern
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+ // Use React Hook Form + Zod resolver
+ const form = useForm&lt;FormData&gt;({
+   resolver: zodResolver(schema),
+   defaultValues: { /* ... */ }
+ });
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Query Pattern
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+ // Use TanStack Query
+ const { data, isLoading } = useQuery({
+   queryKey: ['teachers'],
+   queryFn: () =&gt; api.teachers.list(),
+   staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // 5 minutes
+ });
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## When Writing Code
+ 
+ 1. **Check existing patterns** in the codebase first
+ 2. **Follow RTL-first** principles for all UI code
+ 3. **Use TypeScript strictly** - avoid &#96;any&#96;, use proper types
+ 4. **Add JSDoc** for public APIs
+ 5. **Test in both RTL and LTR** modes
+ 6. **Consider performance** for drag-and-drop interactions
+ 7. **Validate with Zod** at API boundaries
+ 8. **Use logical properties** in Tailwind classes
+ 
+ ## Avoid
+ 
+ - ❌ Using &#96;left-*&#96;, &#96;right-*&#96; in Tailwind (use logical properties)
+ - ❌ Using &#96;any&#96; type (use &#96;unknown&#96; or proper types)
+ - ❌ Mixing server and client state management
+ - ❌ Hardcoding API URLs (use env variables)
+ - ❌ Ignoring RTL layout considerations
+ - ❌ Creating duplicate subject/teacher records (see memory about unique constraints)
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,299 @@
- 
+ # Cursor IDE Setup Guide for Maktab Project
+ 
+ This document provides comprehensive setup instructions for optimizing Cursor IDE for the Maktab timetable application development.
+ 
+ ## 📋 Quick Setup Checklist
+ 
+ - [x] &#96;.cursorrules&#96; file created (project-specific AI rules)
+ - [x] &#96;.vscode/settings.json&#96; configured (editor settings)
+ - [x] &#96;.vscode/extensions.json&#96; created (recommended extensions)
+ - [x] &#96;.prettierrc.json&#96; configured (code formatting)
+ - [ ] Install recommended extensions
+ - [ ] Configure ESLint (when frontend is created)
+ - [ ] Set up Python environment for solver package
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🎯 Configuration Files Created
+ 
+ ### 1. &#96;.cursorrules&#96;
+ **Location:** Root directory  
+ **Purpose:** Provides context and coding guidelines to Cursor AI
+ 
+ **Key Features:**
+ - Project structure and technology stack context
+ - RTL-first development guidelines
+ - TypeScript/React best practices
+ - API integration patterns
+ - Code style conventions
+ 
+ **How to Use:**
+ - Cursor automatically reads this file
+ - AI suggestions will follow these rules
+ - Update as project evolves
+ 
+ ### 2. &#96;.vscode/settings.json&#96;
+ **Location:** &#96;.vscode/settings.json&#96;  
+ **Purpose:** Workspace-specific editor settings
+ 
+ **Key Settings:**
+ - TypeScript configuration (strict mode, inlay hints)
+ - Format on save enabled
+ - Tailwind CSS IntelliSense
+ - Python interpreter path
+ - File exclusions for performance
+ 
+ ### 3. &#96;.vscode/extensions.json&#96;
+ **Location:** &#96;.vscode/extensions.json&#96;  
+ **Purpose:** Recommends essential extensions
+ 
+ **Install Extensions:**
+ 1. Open Extensions view (&#96;Ctrl+Shift+X&#96; / &#96;Cmd+Shift+X&#96;)
+ 2. Search for "Recommended Extensions"
+ 3. Click "Install All" or install individually
+ 
+ ### 4. &#96;.prettierrc.json&#96;
+ **Location:** Root directory  
+ **Purpose:** Code formatting configuration
+ 
+ **Settings:**
+ - Single quotes for JavaScript/TypeScript
+ - 2-space indentation
+ - 100 character line width
+ - LF line endings
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🔧 Manual Setup Steps
+ 
+ ### Step 1: Install Recommended Extensions
+ 
+ Open the Extensions view and install:
+ 
+ **Essential:**
+ - ✅ ESLint (&#96;dbaeumer.vscode-eslint&#96;)
+ - ✅ Prettier (&#96;esbenp.prettier-vscode&#96;)
+ - ✅ Tailwind CSS IntelliSense (&#96;bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss&#96;)
+ 
+ **TypeScript/React:**
+ - ✅ TypeScript Importer (&#96;pmneo.tsimporter&#96;)
+ - ✅ ES7+ React/Redux/React-Native snippets (&#96;dsznajder.es7-react-js-snippets&#96;)
+ - ✅ Auto Rename Tag (&#96;formulahendry.auto-rename-tag&#96;)
+ 
+ **Code Quality:**
+ - ✅ Error Lens (&#96;usernamehw.errorlens&#96;) - Shows errors inline
+ - ✅ Code Spell Checker (&#96;streetsidesoftware.code-spell-checker&#96;)
+ 
+ **Git:**
+ - ✅ GitLens (&#96;eamodio.gitlens&#96;)
+ 
+ **Python (for solver):**
+ - ✅ Python (&#96;ms-python.python&#96;)
+ - ✅ Black Formatter (&#96;ms-python.black-formatter&#96;)
+ - ✅ Pylance (&#96;ms-python.vscode-pylance&#96;)
+ 
+ ### Step 2: Configure ESLint (When Frontend is Created)
+ 
+ Once &#96;packages/web&#96; is set up, create &#96;.eslintrc.json&#96;:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "extends": [
+     "eslint:recommended",
+     "plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended",
+     "plugin:react/recommended",
+     "plugin:react-hooks/recommended",
+     "prettier"
+   ],
+   "parser": "@typescript-eslint/parser",
+   "parserOptions": {
+     "ecmaVersion": 2020,
+     "sourceType": "module",
+     "ecmaFeatures": {
+       "jsx": true
+     }
+   },
+   "plugins": ["@typescript-eslint", "react", "react-hooks"],
+   "rules": {
+     "react/react-in-jsx-scope": "off",
+     "@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types": "off",
+     "@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any": "warn"
+   },
+   "settings": {
+     "react": {
+       "version": "detect"
+     }
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Step 3: Set Up Python Environment (Solver Package)
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ cd packages/solver
+ python3 -m venv .venv
+ source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
+ pip install -r requirements.txt
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Cursor will automatically detect the virtual environment if it's in &#96;packages/solver/.venv/&#96;.
+ 
+ ### Step 4: Configure TypeScript Paths (When Frontend is Created)
+ 
+ In &#96;packages/web/tsconfig.json&#96;, add path aliases:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;json
+ {
+   "compilerOptions": {
+     "baseUrl": ".",
+     "paths": {
+       "@/*": ["./src/*"],
+       "@/components/*": ["./src/components/*"],
+       "@/features/*": ["./src/features/*"],
+       "@/lib/*": ["./src/lib/*"],
+       "@/hooks/*": ["./src/hooks/*"],
+       "@/stores/*": ["./src/stores/*"],
+       "@/i18n/*": ["./src/i18n/*"]
+     }
+   }
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Also configure in &#96;vite.config.ts&#96;:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+ import path from 'path';
+ 
+ export default defineConfig({
+   resolve: {
+     alias: {
+       '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
+     },
+   },
+ });
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🎨 Cursor AI Features to Leverage
+ 
+ ### 1. Chat with Codebase
+ - Use &#96;@&#96; mentions to reference files
+ - Ask questions about architecture
+ - Get explanations of complex code
+ 
+ ### 2. Composer Mode
+ - Multi-file editing
+ - Refactoring across files
+ - Feature implementation
+ 
+ ### 3. Inline Suggestions
+ - Real-time code completions
+ - Follows &#96;.cursorrules&#96; guidelines
+ - Respects RTL-first principles
+ 
+ ### 4. Code Actions
+ - Right-click for AI-powered refactoring
+ - Generate tests
+ - Add documentation
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🚀 Best Practices for Cursor
+ 
+ ### 1. Use Specific Prompts
+ ❌ Bad: "Fix the bug"  
+ ✅ Good: "Fix the duplicate subject creation issue in the subjects feature. Ensure backend unique constraints are enforced and frontend prevents duplicate creates."
+ 
+ ### 2. Reference Architecture Docs
+ When asking about implementation:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ @docs/FRONTEND_ARCHITECTURE.md How should I implement the schedule board component?
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 3. Use File References
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ @packages/api/src/routes/teachers.route.ts How does the teacher API work?
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 4. Context-Aware Requests
+ Mention relevant context:
+ - "Following RTL-first principles, create a form component..."
+ - "Using TanStack Query pattern from the architecture doc, implement..."
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🔍 Troubleshooting
+ 
+ ### TypeScript Errors Not Showing
+ 1. Check &#96;typescript.tsdk&#96; in settings
+ 2. Reload window: &#96;Ctrl+Shift+P&#96; → "Developer: Reload Window"
+ 3. Run: &#96;npm install&#96; in workspace root
+ 
+ ### Prettier Not Formatting
+ 1. Ensure Prettier extension is installed
+ 2. Check &#96;.prettierrc.json&#96; exists
+ 3. Verify file is not in &#96;.prettierignore&#96;
+ 4. Set Prettier as default formatter for file type
+ 
+ ### Tailwind IntelliSense Not Working
+ 1. Install Tailwind CSS IntelliSense extension
+ 2. Ensure &#96;tailwind.config.ts&#96; exists in &#96;packages/web/&#96;
+ 3. Check file associations in settings
+ 
+ ### Python Not Detected
+ 1. Select interpreter: &#96;Ctrl+Shift+P&#96; → "Python: Select Interpreter"
+ 2. Choose &#96;packages/solver/.venv/bin/python&#96;
+ 3. Install Python extension if missing
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 📝 Additional Recommendations
+ 
+ ### 1. Workspace Trust
+ When opening the project, Cursor may ask to trust the workspace. Click "Yes" to enable all features.
+ 
+ ### 2. Git Integration
+ - Enable GitLens for better Git visualization
+ - Configure Git user if not already set
+ 
+ ### 3. Terminal Profiles
+ The settings configure bash as default terminal. Adjust if using zsh or fish.
+ 
+ ### 4. Performance
+ Large monorepos can be slow. The settings exclude:
+ - &#96;node_modules/&#96;
+ - &#96;dist/&#96; and &#96;build/&#96; directories
+ - Python cache files
+ 
+ If still slow, consider:
+ - Using &#96;.cursorignore&#96; for additional exclusions
+ - Limiting file watchers
+ - Using workspace folders instead of full monorepo
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 🎯 Next Steps
+ 
+ 1. ✅ Install recommended extensions
+ 2. ✅ Review &#96;.cursorrules&#96; for project guidelines
+ 3. ✅ Test TypeScript IntelliSense in &#96;packages/api&#96;
+ 4. ⏳ Set up frontend package (&#96;packages/web&#96;) when ready
+ 5. ⏳ Configure ESLint for frontend
+ 6. ⏳ Test RTL layout features
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## 📚 Related Documentation
+ 
+ - &#96;docs/FRONTEND_ARCHITECTURE.md&#96; - Frontend architecture details
+ - &#96;docs/API_FILE_STRUCTURE.md&#96; - Backend API structure
+ - &#96;.cursorrules&#96; - AI coding guidelines
+ - &#96;NAQSH-UI-DESIGN-SYSTEM.md&#96; - Design system reference
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ **Last Updated:** December 2025  
+ **Cursor Version:** Latest  
+ **Project:** Maktab Timetable Application
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

Created Cursor IDE configuration files. Summary:

## Configuration files created

### 1. `.cursorrules` (Root)
- Project context and coding guidelines for Cursor AI
- RTL-first development rules
- TypeScript/React best practices
- Technology stack patterns (TanStack Router, Query, Zustand)
- API integration guidelines
- Code style conventions

### 2. `.vscode/settings.json`
- TypeScript configuration (strict mode, inlay hints)
- Format on save with Prettier
- Tailwind CSS IntelliSense settings
- Python configuration for solver package
- File exclusions for performance
- Language-specific formatters

### 3. `.vscode/extensions.json`
- Recommended extensions list
- Essential: ESLint, Prettier, Tailwind CSS
- TypeScript/React: TypeScript Importer, React snippets
- Python: Python, Black Formatter, Pylance
- Code quality: Error Lens, Spell Checker

### 4. `.prettierrc.json`
- Code formatting rules
- Single quotes, 2-space indentation
- 100 character line width
- File-specific overrides

### 5. `.prettierignore`
- Excludes build outputs, node_modules, etc.

### 6. `CURSOR_SETUP.md`
- Setup guide
- Step-by-step instructions
- Troubleshooting tips
- Best practices for using Cursor AI

## Immediate actions

1. Install recommended extensions:
   - Open Extensions (`Ctrl+Shift+X`)
   - Click "Show Recommended Extensions"
   - Install all (or install individually)

2. Reload Cursor:
   - `Ctrl+Shift+P` → "Developer: Reload Window"
   - This applies the new settings

3. Verify TypeScript:
   - Open any `.ts` file in `packages/api`
   - Check for IntelliSense and type checking

4. Test Prettier:
   - Open a file and save (`Ctrl+S`)
   - It should auto-format

## Key features configured

- RTL-first development: Guidelines in `.cursorrules` for Persian/Dari UI
- TypeScript strict mode: Enabled with helpful inlay hints
- Auto-formatting: Prettier on save
- Tailwind IntelliSense: Configured for logical properties (RTL support)
- Python support: Solver package configured
- Code quality: ESLint integration ready

## What this gives you

1. Better AI suggestions: Cursor follows your project patterns
2. Consistent code style: Auto-formatting with Prettier
3. Type safety: TypeScript IntelliSense and error detection
4. RTL awareness: Guidelines for Persian/Dari UI development
5. Faster development: Tailwind IntelliSense, auto-imports, snippets

## Next steps

When you create the frontend (`packages/web`), I can:
- Set up ESLint configuration
- Configure Vite with path aliases
- Create the initial project structure
- Set up TanStack Router
- Configure Tailwind CSS with RTL support

The configuration is ready. Start building the frontend when you're ready.

```diff
+ import { type ClassValue, clsx } from 'clsx';
+ import { twMerge } from 'tailwind-merge';
+ 
+ /**
+  * Merges Tailwind CSS classes with proper conflict resolution
+  * @param inputs - Class names or class objects
+  * @returns Merged class string
+  */
+ export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
+   return twMerge(clsx(inputs));
+ }
+
```

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,112 @@
- 
+ /**
+  * API client configuration and utilities
+  * @module lib/api
+  */
+ 
+ const API_BASE_URL = import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || 'http://localhost:3000/api';
+ 
+ /**
+  * Base fetch wrapper with error handling
+  */
+ async function fetchAPI&lt;T&gt;(
+   endpoint: string,
+   options?: RequestInit
+ ): Promise&lt;T&gt; {
+   const url = &#96;${API_BASE_URL}${endpoint}&#96;;
+   
+   const response = await fetch(url, {
+     ...options,
+     headers: {
+       'Content-Type': 'application/json',
+       ...options?.headers,
+     },
+   });
+ 
+   if (!response.ok) {
+     const error = await response.json().catch(() =&gt; ({
+       message: response.statusText,
+     }));
+     throw new Error(error.message || &#96;HTTP error! status: ${response.status}&#96;);
+   }
+ 
+   return response.json();
+ }
+ 
+ /**
+  * API client with resource-specific methods
+  */
+ export const api = {
+   teachers: {
+     list: () =&gt; fetchAPI&lt;unknown[]&gt;('/teachers'),
+     get: (id: number) =&gt; fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;(&#96;/teachers/${id}&#96;),
+     create: (data: unknown) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;('/teachers', {
+         method: 'POST',
+         body: JSON.stringify(data),
+       }),
+     update: (id: number, data: unknown) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;(&#96;/teachers/${id}&#96;, {
+         method: 'PUT',
+         body: JSON.stringify(data),
+       }),
+     delete: (id: number) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;void&gt;(&#96;/teachers/${id}&#96;, {
+         method: 'DELETE',
+       }),
+   },
+   subjects: {
+     list: () =&gt; fetchAPI&lt;unknown[]&gt;('/subjects'),
+     get: (id: number) =&gt; fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;(&#96;/subjects/${id}&#96;),
+     create: (data: unknown) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;('/subjects', {
+         method: 'POST',
+         body: JSON.stringify(data),
+       }),
+     update: (id: number, data: unknown) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;(&#96;/subjects/${id}&#96;, {
+         method: 'PUT',
+         body: JSON.stringify(data),
+       }),
+     delete: (id: number) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;void&gt;(&#96;/subjects/${id}&#96;, {
+         method: 'DELETE',
+       }),
+   },
+   classes: {
+     list: () =&gt; fetchAPI&lt;unknown[]&gt;('/classes'),
+     get: (id: number) =&gt; fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;(&#96;/classes/${id}&#96;),
+     create: (data: unknown) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;('/classes', {
+         method: 'POST',
+         body: JSON.stringify(data),
+       }),
+     update: (id: number, data: unknown) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;unknown&gt;(&#96;/classes/${id}&#96;, {
+         method: 'PUT',
+         body: JSON.stringify(data),
+       }),
+     delete: (id: number) =&gt;
+       fetchAPI&lt;void&gt;(&#96;/classes/${id}&#96;, {
+         method: 'DELETE',
+       }),
+   },
+   rooms: {
```

```diff
+   }
+ }
+ 
+ // Create a query client with default options
+ const queryClient = new QueryClient({
+   defaultOptions: {
+     queries: {
+       staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // 5 minutes for reference data
+       gcTime: 30 * 60 * 1000, // 30 minutes (formerly cacheTime)
+       retry: 3,
+       refetchOnWindowFocus: true,
+     },
+   },
+ });
+ 
+ createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
+   &lt;StrictMode&gt;
+     &lt;QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}&gt;
+       &lt;RouterProvider router={router} /&gt;
+       {import.meta.env.DEV &amp;&amp; &lt;ReactQueryDevtools initialIsOpen={false} /&gt;}
+     &lt;/QueryClientProvider&gt;
+   &lt;/StrictMode&gt;
+ );
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

Lines added: 49, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,49 @@
- 
+ import * as React from 'react';
+ import { Slot } from '@radix-ui/react-slot';
+ import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority';
+ import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
+ 
+ const buttonVariants = cva(
+   'inline-flex items-center justify-center whitespace-nowrap rounded-md text-sm font-medium ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50',
+   {
+     variants: {
+       variant: {
+         default: 'bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary-hover',
+         destructive: 'bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground hover:bg-destructive/90',
+         outline: 'border border-input bg-background hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground',
+         secondary: 'bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary-hover',
+         ghost: 'hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground',
+         link: 'text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline',
+       },
+       size: {
+         default: 'h-10 px-4 py-2',
+         sm: 'h-9 rounded-md px-3',
+         lg: 'h-11 rounded-md px-8',
+         icon: 'h-10 w-10',
+       },
+     },
+     defaultVariants: {
+       variant: 'default',
+       size: 'default',
+     },
+   }
+ );
+ 
+ export interface ButtonProps
+   extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes&lt;HTMLButtonElement&gt;,
+     VariantProps&lt;typeof buttonVariants&gt; {
+   asChild?: boolean;
+ }
+ 
+ const Button = React.forwardRef&lt;HTMLButtonElement, ButtonProps&gt;(
+   ({ className, variant, size, asChild = false, ...props }, ref) =&gt; {
+     const Comp = asChild ? Slot : 'button';
+     return (
+       &lt;Comp className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))} ref={ref} {...props} /&gt;
+     );
+   }
+ );
+ Button.displayName = 'Button';
+ 
+ export { Button, buttonVariants };
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,130 @@
- 
+ # Maktab Web Frontend
+ 
+ Frontend application for the Maktab timetable scheduling system.
+ 
+ ## Technology Stack
+ 
+ - **React 19.2.3** - UI framework
+ - **Vite 7.3.0** - Build tool and dev server
+ - **TanStack Router 1.141.6** - Type-safe file-based routing
+ - **TanStack Query 5.90.12** - Server state management
+ - **Zustand 5.0.9** - Client state management
+ - **Tailwind CSS 4.1.18** - Utility-first CSS (RTL-first)
+ - **React Hook Form + Zod** - Form validation
+ - **@dnd-kit** - Drag-and-drop for schedule editing
+ - **react-i18next** - Internationalization
+ 
+ ## Getting Started
+ 
+ ### Prerequisites
+ 
+ - Node.js 18+ and npm
+ - Backend API running on &#96;http://localhost:3000&#96;
+ 
+ ### Installation
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ # Install dependencies
+ npm install
+ 
+ # Start development server
+ npm run dev
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ The app will be available at &#96;http://localhost:5173&#96;
+ 
+ ### Environment Variables
+ 
+ Create a &#96;.env&#96; file (see &#96;.env.example&#96;):
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;env
+ VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000/api
+ VITE_APP_VERSION=1.0.0
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Project Structure
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ src/
+ ├── components/        # Reusable UI components
+ │   ├── ui/           # shadcn/ui components
+ │   ├── layout/       # Shell, Sidebar, Header
+ │   └── schedule/     # Schedule grid components
+ ├── features/         # Feature modules
+ │   ├── teachers/
+ │   ├── classes/
+ │   ├── subjects/
+ │   ├── rooms/
+ │   ├── timetable/
+ │   └── wizard/
+ ├── hooks/            # Custom React hooks
+ ├── lib/              # Utilities and API client
+ ├── routes/           # TanStack Router routes
+ ├── stores/           # Zustand stores
+ ├── schemas/          # Zod validation schemas
+ ├── i18n/             # Translation files
+ ├── styles/           # Global styles
+ └── types/            # TypeScript types
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Development
+ 
+ ### Available Scripts
+ 
+ - &#96;npm run dev&#96; - Start development server
+ - &#96;npm run build&#96; - Build for production
+ - &#96;npm run preview&#96; - Preview production build
+ - &#96;npm run lint&#96; - Run ESLint
+ - &#96;npm run type-check&#96; - Type check without emitting
+ 
+ ### Code Style
+ 
+ - Follow &#96;.cursorrules&#96; for AI-assisted development
+ - Use Prettier for formatting (auto-format on save)
+ - Follow ESLint rules
+ - Use RTL-first approach (logical properties in Tailwind)
+ 
+ ### Path Aliases
+ 
+ Use path aliases for cleaner imports:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+ import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
+ import { useUIStore } from '@/stores/uiStore';
+ import { api } from '@/lib/api';
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## RTL Support
+ 
+ The application is RTL-first for Persian/Dari:
+ 
+ - Use logical properties: &#96;ms-4&#96;, &#96;me-4&#96;, &#96;text-start&#96;, etc.
+ - Never use &#96;left-*&#96;, &#96;right-*&#96;, &#96;ml-*&#96;, &#96;mr-*&#96; for layout
+ - HTML root has &#96;dir="rtl"&#96; and &#96;lang="fa"&#96;
+ 
+ ## API Integration
+ 
+ The API client is configured in &#96;src/lib/api.ts&#96;. Use TanStack Query for data fetching:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+ import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query';
+ import { api } from '@/lib/api';
+ 
+ const { data, isLoading } = useQuery({
+   queryKey: ['teachers'],
+   queryFn: () =&gt; api.teachers.list(),
+ });
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Building
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ npm run build
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Output will be in the &#96;dist/&#96; directory.
+ 
+ ## License
+ 
+ MIT
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,161 @@
- 
+ # Frontend Setup Instructions
+ 
+ ## Quick Start
+ 
+ 1. **Install Dependencies**
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    cd packages/web
+    npm install
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ 2. **Set Up Environment Variables**
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    cp .env.example .env
+    # Edit .env if needed (defaults should work)
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ 3. **Generate TanStack Router Route Tree**
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    # This will be done automatically by TanStack Router
+    # But you can run manually if needed:
+    npx @tanstack/router-cli generate
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ 4. **Start Development Server**
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+    npm run dev
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+    The app will be available at &#96;http://localhost:5173&#96;
+ 
+ ## What's Already Set Up
+ 
+ ✅ **Configuration Files**
+ - &#96;package.json&#96; - All dependencies configured
+ - &#96;tsconfig.json&#96; - TypeScript with path aliases
+ - &#96;vite.config.ts&#96; - Vite with React plugin
+ - &#96;tailwind.config.ts&#96; - Tailwind CSS 4 with Naqsh design system
+ - &#96;.eslintrc.json&#96; - ESLint for React/TypeScript
+ - &#96;.prettierrc.json&#96; - Code formatting (inherited from root)
+ 
+ ✅ **Project Structure**
+ - Basic directory structure created
+ - Route files (&#96;__root.tsx&#96;, &#96;index.tsx&#96;)
+ - Core utilities (&#96;lib/utils.ts&#96;, &#96;lib/api.ts&#96;)
+ - UI store (&#96;stores/uiStore.ts&#96;)
+ - i18n setup (&#96;i18n/index.ts&#96;)
+ - Global styles with Naqsh design system
+ 
+ ✅ **Core Features**
+ - TanStack Router configured
+ - TanStack Query configured
+ - Zustand store setup
+ - Basic Button component (shadcn/ui pattern)
+ - RTL-first HTML structure
+ 
+ ## Next Steps
+ 
+ ### 1. Install Dependencies
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ cd packages/web
+ npm install
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 2. Add Fonts (Optional but Recommended)
+ Place font files in &#96;public/fonts/&#96;:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ public/fonts/
+ ├── vazirmatn/
+ │   ├── Vazirmatn-Regular.woff2
+ │   ├── Vazirmatn-Medium.woff2
+ │   └── Vazirmatn-Bold.woff2
+ ├── inter/
+ │   └── Inter-Variable.woff2
+ └── jetbrains-mono/
+     └── JetBrainsMono-Variable.woff2
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Fonts are already configured in &#96;src/styles/globals.css&#96;. If you don't add fonts, the browser will use fallbacks.
+ 
+ ### 3. Generate Route Tree
+ TanStack Router will auto-generate &#96;src/routeTree.gen.ts&#96; on first run. If you need to generate manually:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ npx @tanstack/router-cli generate
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### 4. Start Building Features
+ 
+ Now you can start building:
+ - Feature modules in &#96;src/features/&#96;
+ - Layout components in &#96;src/components/layout/&#96;
+ - Schedule components in &#96;src/components/schedule/&#96;
+ - Custom hooks in &#96;src/hooks/&#96;
+ 
+ ## Troubleshooting
+ 
+ ### TypeScript Errors
+ If you see TypeScript errors about missing types:
+ 1. Make sure &#96;npm install&#96; completed successfully
+ 2. Restart TypeScript server: &#96;Ctrl+Shift+P&#96; → "TypeScript: Restart TS Server"
+ 
+ ### Vite Not Starting
+ - Check that port 5173 is available
+ - Verify Node.js version is 18+
+ - Check for syntax errors in config files
+ 
+ ### Tailwind Not Working
+ - Ensure &#96;tailwindcss&#96; is installed
+ - Check &#96;tailwind.config.ts&#96; exists
+ - Verify &#96;postcss.config.js&#96; exists
+ - Check that &#96;globals.css&#96; imports Tailwind directives
+ 
+ ### TanStack Router Errors
+ - Route tree will be auto-generated on first run
+ - If you see route errors, run: &#96;npx @tanstack/router-cli generate&#96;
+ 
+ ## Development Workflow
+ 
+ 1. **Create a new route:**
+    - Add file in &#96;src/routes/&#96; (e.g., &#96;src/routes/about.tsx&#96;)
+    - Route tree auto-generates
+    - Access at &#96;/about&#96;
+ 
+ 2. **Create a feature:**
+    - Create directory in &#96;src/features/&#96; (e.g., &#96;src/features/teachers/&#96;)
+    - Add components, hooks, types, etc.
+    - Import using &#96;@/features/teachers/...&#96;
+ 
+ 3. **Add a UI component:**
+    - Use shadcn/ui pattern (see &#96;src/components/ui/button.tsx&#96;)
+    - Place in &#96;src/components/ui/&#96;
+    - Export from &#96;src/components/ui/index.ts&#96; (create if needed)
+ 
+ 4. **Use API:**
+    - Import from &#96;@/lib/api&#96;
+    - Use with TanStack Query:
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;typescript
+    import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query';
+    import { api } from '@/lib/api';
+    
+    const { data } = useQuery({
+      queryKey: ['teachers'],
+      queryFn: () =&gt; api.teachers.list(),
+    });
+    &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Testing
+ 
+ When you're ready to add tests:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ npm install -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ## Building for Production
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ npm run build
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Output will be in &#96;dist/&#96; directory, ready for Electron packaging.
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

### Configuration files created

1. **package.json** - Dependencies configured (React 19, Vite, TanStack Router/Query, etc.)
2. **tsconfig.json** - TypeScript with path aliases (`@/*`, `@/components/*`, etc.)
3. **vite.config.ts** - Vite with React plugin and path resolution
4. **tailwind.config.ts** - Tailwind CSS 4 with Naqsh design system colors
5. **postcss.config.js** - PostCSS configuration
6. **.eslintrc.json** - ESLint for React/TypeScript
7. **index.html** - HTML with RTL setup (`dir="rtl" lang="fa"`)

### Project structure created

```
packages/web/
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── ui/          # shadcn/ui components (Button example)
│   │   ├── layout/      # Layout components (placeholder)
│   │   └── schedule/    # Schedule components (placeholder)
│   ├── features/        # Feature modules (placeholder)
│   ├── hooks/          # Custom hooks (placeholder)
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── api.ts      # API client
│   │   └── utils.ts    # Utility functions (cn helper)
│   ├── routes/
│   │   ├── __root.tsx  # Root route
│   │   └── index.tsx   # Home page
│   ├── stores/
│   │   └── uiStore.ts  # Zustand UI store
│   ├── i18n/
│   │   └── index.ts    # i18n configuration
│   ├── styles/
│   │   └── globals.css  # Tailwind + Naqsh design system
│   ├── main.tsx        # Entry point
│   └── vite-env.d.ts   # Vite type definitions
├── public/             # Static assets (fonts will go here)
└── [config files]
```

### Features configured

- TanStack Router: File-based routing with root and index routes
- TanStack Query: Configured with 5min staleTime, 30min cacheTime
- Zustand: UI store with theme, language, sidebar state
- Tailwind CSS: Naqsh design system variables, RTL support
- i18n: react-i18next setup (Persian/English)
- API client: Base structure for teachers, subjects, classes, rooms
- TypeScript: Strict mode, path aliases, type safety

### Next steps

1. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   cd packages/web
   npm install
   ```

2. Start development server:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

3. Optional: Add fonts to `public/fonts/` (see SETUP.md)

### Notes

- TypeScript errors about `i18next` are expected until `npm install` runs
- TanStack Router will auto-generate `routeTree.gen.ts` on first run
- All path aliases are configured and ready to use
- RTL-first approach is set up in HTML and Tailwind config

### Documentation

- `README.md` - Project overview and structure
- `SETUP.md` - Detailed setup instructions and troubleshooting

The frontend is ready for development. Install dependencies and start building features.
